_source: Room-held-out train/val protocol for InventoryFlex ML experiments (docs/19 §2.3, §4 Phase 0).
description: Entire rooms are assigned to train or val — never split items within a room. Use train rooms for fitting (fine-tune, threshold tuning, pseudo-label mining) and val rooms for held-out metrics. Detection bbox eval (ML-E11) and room-specific failure modes concentrate on the two val rooms.

rationale.val_rooms: Bathroom and Reception & Open Plan Kitchen are the ML-E11 bbox-labelling targets and the best/worst YOLOE recall rooms in docs/13 — held out so detection fine-tune (ML-E12) and mAP eval do not leak train signal.
rationale.train_rooms: Four remaining rooms (33 notable gold items) supply describe/detection training signal without overlapping the bbox gold set.

experiments.ML-E11: bbox labels only in val_rooms (+ optional train pseudo-labels)
experiments.ML-E12: fine-tune on train_rooms; mAP on val_rooms labels_boxes.json
experiments.ML-E10: detector recall reported overall and per split
experiments.describe_eval: run_eval.py on full fixture for CI; ML ablations should report train vs val separately

usage: Filter rooms with train_rooms / val_rooms before exporting crops, computing metrics, or selecting photos. Do not move rooms between splits without updating labels_boxes.json and re-benchmarking.
